DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-20-2018 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Gene F. Williams, Esq. of Makarem & Associates, T:310.312.0299 ext. 307, for plaintiff, by Court Call;
No other appearance.

RULING: There being no objection, the Court adopts the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT CRAIG PEARCE's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT as follows:

Defendant Craig Pearce's demurrer to all causes of action alleged in the second amended class action complaint filed by plaintiff Johnny Williams is sustained without leave to amend as to all causes of action. Mr. Williams, who ceased his employment with defendants prior to the effective date of Labor Code 558.1, lacks standing to sue Mr. Pearce based on that statute, even if, which is at best unclear, section 558.1 permits the imposition of liability in a lawsuit brought by a party other than the Labor Commissioner. Mr. Pearce's request to propound discovery in an effort to locate a plaintiff with standing to sue Mr. Pearce is denied per CVS Pharmacy, Inc. v. Superior Court (2015) 241 Cal. App. 4th 300 and Starbucks Corporation v. Superior Court (2011) 194 Cal. App. 4th 820. Just as in those cases, application of the Parris balancing test in this case shows that the potential for abuse of the class action procedure outweighs any likely benefit to the class. It has been many years since this case was filed and the fact that no other possible named plaintiff has been identified or discovered is a strong indication that few if any exist. Defendants' former employees know whether they were victims of any Labor Code violations and have been free to pursue claims on their own. Because the requested discovery impinges on those employees' privacy rights, some showing should be made that the discovery would result in any useful information and why that information was not obtained earlier. Mr. Williams fails to make such a showing. Accordingly, there is no basis to grant Mr. Williams' request to take discovery to try to locate a "head" for this "headless class action."

ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: torreanom@aol.com; mtorreano@sftc.org.
